subject: Science
title: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

questions.text: The thin membrane through which light enters the eye is called:
questions.explanation: The cornea is the transparent spherical membrane at the front of the eye through which light enters.

questions.text: The amount of light entering the eye is controlled and regulated by the:
questions.explanation: The iris is a dark muscular diaphragm that controls the size of the pupil, which in turn regulates the amount of light.

questions.text: The eye lens forms which type of image on the retina?
questions.explanation: The crystalline lens forms a real and inverted image of objects on the retina.

questions.text: The ability of the eye lens to adjust its focal length is called:
questions.explanation: Power of accommodation is the ability of the ciliary muscles to change the curvature of the lens to see objects at different distances.

questions.text: The least distance of distinct vision for a young adult with normal vision is about:
questions.explanation: 25 cm is the near point of the eye, the minimum distance at which an object can be seen clearly without strain.

questions.text: A person who can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant objects distinctly is suffering from:
questions.explanation: Myopia (near-sightedness) occurs when the image of a distant object is formed in front of the retina.

questions.text: Myopia is corrected by using a lens of which type?
questions.explanation: A concave lens of suitable power diverges light rays so that the image forms correctly on the retina.

questions.text: Hypermetropia (far-sightedness) is corrected by using a:
questions.explanation: A convex lens provides the additional focusing power needed to bring the image onto the retina.

questions.text: The defect of vision that arises due to the gradual weakening of ciliary muscles with aging is:
questions.explanation: Presbyopia occurs in old age as the eye lens loses its flexibility and the near point recedes.

questions.text: The splitting of white light into its component colors is called:
questions.explanation: Dispersion occurs because different colors of light bend through different angles while passing through a prism.

questions.text: In the spectrum of white light produced by a glass prism, which color bends the most?
questions.explanation: Violet light has the shortest wavelength and undergoes maximum deviation.

questions.text: The twinkling of stars is due to:
questions.explanation: The varying temperature and density of air layers cause the apparent position of the star to fluctuate.

questions.text: The sun appears visible to us about 2 minutes before the actual sunrise due to:
questions.explanation: Atmospheric refraction bends the sunlight towards the Earth, allowing us to see the sun even when it is below the horizon.

questions.text: The sky appears blue because:
options: Blue light is absorbed by atmosphere
Atmosphere scatters blue light more than red
Water in oceans reflects blue sky
questions.explanation: Molecules in the air scatter light with shorter wavelengths (blue) more effectively than longer wavelengths (red).

questions.text: The scattering of light by colloidal particles is known as:
questions.explanation: Tyndall effect makes the path of a beam of light visible when it passes through a colloidal solution or dusty air.

questions.text: The danger signal lights are red in color because:
options: Red is scattered the least by fog or smoke
Red is most soothing to eyes
questions.explanation: Red has the longest wavelength in the visible spectrum and is least scattered, making it visible from long distances.

questions.text: A rainbow is a natural spectrum appearing in the sky after rain. It is caused by:
questions.explanation: Water droplets act as tiny prisms that refract, disperse, and then internally reflect the sunlight.

questions.text: For an astronaut in outer space, the sky appears:
questions.explanation: There is no atmosphere in space to scatter light, so the sky looks dark/black.

questions.text: The point in the eye where the optic nerve leaves and no vision is possible is the:
questions.explanation: The blind spot lacks light-sensitive cells (rods and cones), so no image is perceived there.

questions.text: The light-sensitive cells in the retina that respond to the intensity of light are:
questions.explanation: Rod cells are sensitive to dim light, while cones are sensitive to color and bright light.

questions.text: When ciliary muscles are relaxed, the eye lens becomes:
questions.explanation: Relaxed muscles make the lens thin, allowing the eye to see distant objects clearly.

questions.text: Which part of the eye is responsible for the color of a person's eyes?
questions.explanation: The iris contains pigments that give the eye its characteristic color (blue, brown, green, etc.).

questions.text: The clear fluid filled in the space between the cornea and the eye lens is:
questions.explanation: Aqueous humor maintains intraocular pressure and nourishes the front of the eye.

questions.text: The angle between the incident ray and the emergent ray in a prism is called:
questions.explanation: The peculiar shape of the prism makes the emergent ray bend at an angle to the direction of the incident ray.

questions.text: A person needs a lens of power -4.5 D for correction of her vision. The defect she has is:
questions.explanation: Negative power indicates a concave (diverging) lens, which is used to correct myopia.

questions.text: Why do planets not twinkle like stars?
options: They are point sources of light
They are closer to Earth and act as extended sources
They have no light of their own
questions.explanation: Planets are seen as a collection of point sources; fluctuations in light from different points cancel each other out.

questions.text: The condition where the crystalline lens of people at old age becomes milky and cloudy is:
questions.explanation: Cataract causes partial or complete loss of vision and can be restored through surgery.

questions.text: Who was the first to use a glass prism to obtain the spectrum of sunlight?
questions.explanation: Isaac Newton used two prisms to show that white light is made of seven colors and cannot be further split.

questions.text: Very fine particles in the atmosphere scatter mainly:
questions.explanation: Fine particles scatter shorter wavelengths (blue) more, while larger particles scatter longer wavelengths or all wavelengths (appearing white).

questions.text: At noon the sun appears white because:
options: The sun is coldest at noon
Light has to travel less distance and scattering is minimum
questions.explanation: Since the sun is overhead, light travels a shorter path through the atmosphere, and very little scattering occurs.

questions.text: The persistence of vision for a human eye is about:
questions.explanation: The image on the retina persists for about 1/16th of a second, which is the basis for motion pictures.

questions.text: The near point of a hypermetropic eye is:
questions.explanation: In hypermetropia, the person has difficulty seeing nearby objects as the near point recedes away from 25 cm.

questions.text: In the VIBGYOR sequence of colors, which has the highest wavelength?
questions.explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength and Violet has the shortest.

questions.text: The far point of a myopic eye is:
questions.explanation: For a myopic eye, the far point is not infinity but comes closer to the eye.

questions.text: A person using bifocal lenses likely suffers from:
questions.explanation: Bifocal lenses have a concave upper part for distant vision and a convex lower part for reading.

questions.text: The jelly-like transparent material of the eye lens is composed of:
questions.explanation: The crystalline lens is a fibrous, jelly-like protein structure.

questions.text: The fluid filled in the space between the eye lens and the retina is:
questions.explanation: Vitreous humor is a transparent gel that supports the shape of the eyeball.

questions.text: Rainbows are always formed in a direction ________ to that of the Sun.
questions.explanation: To see a rainbow, the sun must be behind the observer and rain must be in front.

questions.text: Due to atmospheric refraction, the day is lengthened by approximately:
questions.explanation: Advanced sunrise (2 min) and delayed sunset (2 min) add up to 4 minutes total.

questions.text: The change in the focal length of an eye lens is caused by the action of the:
questions.explanation: Ciliary muscles change the curvature of the lens, thereby changing its focal length.
